title: Drones Deliver Hope in the Sky
story: Imagine vaccines flying through the air like tiny superheroes! That's what happened in 2018 when a special drone took off on December 19. Its mission? To deliver life-saving vaccines to the island of Erromango in Vanuatu. UNICEF, a group that helps children worldwide, used this flying robot to reach people in hard-to-get places. It was the first time ever that drones were used to bring vaccines to those who needed them most.
activity_question: What organization used a drone to deliver vaccines?
follow_up_question: Why do you think drones might be helpful for delivering medical supplies to islands?

title: Underwater Wonder: The Jellyfish Roundabout
story: Beneath the waves, a marvel of engineering came to life in 2020. The world's first undersea roundabout, nicknamed 'the Jellyfish', opened on December 19. Part of a tunnel network stretching nearly seven miles, it connects islands in the Faroe Islands. Drivers experience a magical moment as they pass through, surrounded by illuminated artwork depicting graceful sea creatures.
activity_question: What is the nickname of the world's first undersea roundabout?
bonus_fact: The stunning artwork in the roundabout was created by Faroese artist Tróndur Patursson.

title: The Birth of a Musical Legend
story: A piano prodigy was born in Bogalusa, Louisiana in 1918. Henry Roeland Byrd, later known as Professor Longhair, would grow up to revolutionize music. His unique blend of boogie-woogie, New Orleans R&B, and mambo created unforgettable tunes like 'Tipitina' and 'Go To The Mardis Gras'. Professor Longhair's December 19 birthday marked the start of a remarkable musical journey.

title: The Voice of Wrestling: 'Mean' Gene Okerlund
story: Lights, camera, action! On December 19, 1942, a legend was born. 'Mean' Gene Okerlund entered the world in Brookings, South Dakota. He grew up to become the iconic voice of professional wrestling. With his smooth style and quick wit, Gene interviewed the biggest stars in WWF and WCW. His microphone skills made him a beloved figure in the ring and beyond.
activity_question: What was 'Mean' Gene Okerlund's profession?
bonus_fact: Despite his nickname, 'Mean' Gene was known for being kind and professional behind the scenes.
